ekern_transcription.label: Full kern transcription with embedded metadata
ekern_transcription.expectation: Metadata and structure expected to succeed; exact pitches may hallucinate. Output is directly renderable via verovio.
ekern_transcription.prompt:
Transcribe this piano sheet music into a complete **kern file. Output ONLY the kern file — no prose, no explanation, no code fences.

RULES:
- Every data row has exactly TWO tab-separated columns: left=bass clef, right=treble clef.
- !!LO: lines and *> lines are STANDALONE lines with NO tabs — they are NOT columns.
- Barlines must also have two columns: =1<TAB>=1

CONCRETE EXAMPLE (TAB = actual tab character between the two columns):
!!!OTL: Title exactly as printed
!!!COM: Full composer line exactly as printed (e.g. 'Composed by ROBERT D. BIDDLE.')
!!!YEC: Copyright line if visible, else omit
**kern TAB **kern
*clefF4 TAB *clefG2
*k[] TAB *k[]           <- key signature: *k[]=C, *k[b-]=F, *k[f#]=G, etc.
*M3/4 TAB *M3/4
!!LO:TX,t=INTRO.,a      <- section label as plain text (NOT *>label)
!!LO:TX,t=Andante.,a
!!LO:DY,t=pp,a
=1 TAB =1
4FF 4C TAB 4r
4F 4c 4a TAB 4a
4F 4c 4a TAB 4a
!!LO:DY,t=cres.,a
=2 TAB =2
...
*k[b-] TAB *k[b-]       <- emit new *k[] here if key changes mid-piece
!!LO:TX,t=WALTZ.,a      <- section label for new section
!!LO:DY,t=p,a
=9 TAB =9
4CC 4C TAB 4e
...
*- TAB *-

PITCH GUIDE:
- Uppercase = below middle C: C D E F G A B (one octave below)
  Double uppercase = two octaves below: CC DD FF GG (e.g. CC = low C)
- Lowercase = above middle C: c d e f g a b
  Double lowercase = two octaves above: cc dd ee ff gg
- Durations: 1=whole 2=half 4=quarter 8=eighth 16=sixteenth
- Rests: r (e.g. 4r = quarter rest)
- Beaming: L=beam start, J=beam end (e.g. 8cL 8dJ)
- Chords: space-separated (e.g. 4C 4E 4G = C major chord)
- Repeat barline: =:|!

Now transcribe the actual score image. Read the title, composer, copyright, section names, key, tempo, and dynamics directly from the image. If the key changes mid-piece, emit the new *k[] record at that point. Transcribe as many measures as you can read accurately.
ekern_transcription.response:
